title: From Ease To Difficulty
study: A Study In The Life Of Joseph
scriptureForWeek: GENESIS 39:6-20 So Potiphar gave Joseph complete administrative responsibility over everything he owned. With Joseph there, he didn’t worry about a thing—except what kind of food to eat! Joseph was a very handsome and well-built young man, and Potiphar’s wife soon began to look at him lustfully. “Come and sleep with me,” she demanded. But Joseph refused. “Look,” he told her, “My master trusts me with everything in his entire household. No one here has more authority than I do. He has held back nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. How could I do such a wicked thing? It would be a great sin against God.” She kept putting pressure on Joseph Day after day, but he refused to sleep with her, and he kept out of her way as much as possible. One day, however, no one else was around when he went in to do his work. She came and grabbed him by his cloak, demanding, “Come on, sleep with me!” Joseph tore himself away, but he left his cloak in her hand as he ran from the house. When she saw that she was holding his cloak and he had fled, she called out to her servants. Soon all the men came running. “Look!” she said. “My husband has brought this Hebrew slave here to make fools of us! He came into my room to rape me, but I screamed. When he heard me scream, he ran outside and got away, but he left his cloak behind with me.” Potiphar was furious when he heard his wife’s story about how Joseph had treated her. So he took Joseph and threw him into the prison where the king’s prisoners were held, and there he remained.
weekTheme: Boys yield, Men resist This week, we will look at something every young man has to learn how to handle: the difficulty of temptation. Facing temptation is something that never goes away. It morphs and changes in every season of life, and we must learn to be vigilant. After Jesus resisted the devil in Luke’s gospel, it says that the devil left him until a more opportune time. Learning to resist temptation as a young man is going to set you up for a life of joy, obedience, and resistance that honors God and unleashes his blessing in your life.

days.title: Temptation Is Intrusive
days.scripture: GENESIS 39:6-7 6 So Potiphar gave Joseph complete administrative responsibility over everything he owned. With Joseph there, he didn’t worry about a thing—except what kind of food to eat! Joseph was a very handsome and well-built young man, 7 and Potiphar’s wife soon began to look at him lustfully. “Come and sleep with me,” she demanded.
days.reflection: Just when it looks like things are settling down and Joseph is getting some of his old favor back, BOOM - something comes at him that is going to be a source of unreal pain. TEMPTATION Temptation is one of the central things that a young man has to learn to fight. You need to know temptation never fights fair. You need to know that temptation is intrusive. This means that it is going to barge its way into your life without asking. Temptation will not ask your permission, wait until you feel strong enough to resist it, or ask for a convenient time. Temptation sneaks in and seeks to take over your life. Think about how intense and challenging this must have been for Joseph. He is working hard to honor God and make the most of his difficult circumstance. God is with him, and he is being recognized as someone who is trustworthy and responsible. And now temptation is coming along and seeking to destroy everything he has been faithful to build. That temptation is in the form of a beautiful woman, his boss’s wife. Joseph was young, lonely, and really vulnerable in that environment. He could have given in, feeling like he deserved affection and attention. But instead, he realized this was an intrusion to his relationship with God and could sabotage his calling. He said no. We see this same strategy at the beginning of the human story. Satan comes along and tempts Adam and Eve. He came into the garden without permission (a real intruder) and began to tempt Adam and Eve to disobey God's commands. Adam and Eve didn’t invite him in. He just came in. Temptation always seeks to throw us off God's plans for our lives. The book of James in the New Testament gives us a clue. It says this: "Dear brothers and sisters, when troubles of any kind come your way, consider it an opportunity for great joy. For you know that when your faith is tested, your endurance has a chance to grow. So let it grow, for when your endurance is fully developed, you will be perfect and complete, needing nothing.” James 1:2-4. That’s how temptation works. It intrudes in our lives. Like someone breaking into a house and seeking to take what doesn’t belong to them, temptation can break into your heart and mind and seek to take your confidence, relationship with God, and closeness with your family. This can cause you to lie, hide, and cover things up because of shame. Over the years, men have noticed there are particular times when temptation seeks to intrude. This means you have to be really aware in these moments. They conveniently spell HALT! Like a soldier called to guard a door who says when someone approaches - "HALT! Who goes there?" HALT Hungry Angry Lonely Tired When we are hungry, our bodies want physical satisfaction. When we are angry, we want to dull our pain. When we are lonely, we want comfort, and when we are tired, we want relief. Joseph had to deal with constant intrusions from temptation. You will too. Learning how to overcome it is a key step on the path to becoming a man.
questions: Look back over the things mentioned in HALT. Have you noticed you are more prone to temptation when you are hungry, angry, lonely or tired? Take a moment to think about it. Then see if you can share an example of being really tempted in one of these states.
What do you think it felt like for Joseph to have to be around this woman all the time? Have you ever been stuck in a situation where you were constantly tempted?
Ask the person doing this study with you to share about a moment when they have experienced temptation.

thoughts.verse: The serpent was the shrewdest of all the wild animals the LORD God had made. One day he asked the woman, “Did God really say you must not eat the fruit from any of the trees in the garden?”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to help you with any temptation you are currently facing. Jesus loves to help us when we pray like this. After all, “Lead us not into temptation” is part of the Lord's prayer.

days.title: Temptation Is Constant
days.scripture: GENESIS 39:7, 10 7 “Come and sleep with me,” she demanded. 10 She kept putting pressure on Joseph day after day, but he refused to sleep with her, and he kept out of her way as much as possible.
days.reflection: In a man's story, resisting temptation will have to become a way of life. This is because temptation is not a one-off event. Naive men think we live in a safe world where we are occasionally tempted, but wise men understand we live in a world of temptation where we occasionally have peace. That’s because we are in a spiritual battle, not in a spiritual amusement park. You have an enemy who hates you and will keep the pressure on to make you fall. God will help us, but we must understand the reality of the battle we are in. Joseph learned how to live his life surrounded by constant temptation. Potiphar’s wife was aggressive and persistent. It says “she kept putting pressure on him day after day.” Imagine how hard that must have been for Joseph to deal with all the time. Every time he saw this woman, she was trying to put the moves on him. He could not let his guard down, because she would be there waiting for him. Constant temptation requires constant awareness. The apostle Paul wrote: "If you think you are standing strong, be careful not to fall. The temptations in your life are no different from what others experience. And God is faithful. He will not allow the temptation to be more than you can stand. When you are tempted, he will show you a way out so that you can endure." (1 Corinthians 10:12-13) We must be aware that Satan seeks to tempt us. In fact he is called “The Tempter.” In 1 Thessalonians 3:5, the apostle Paul says, “I was afraid that the tempter had gotten the best of you and that our work had been useless.” We must also be aware of our own desires that can tempt us. James writes, "When tempted, no one should say, 'God is tempting me.' For God cannot be tempted by evil, nor does he tempt anyone; but each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed. Then, after desire has conceived, it gives birth to sin; and sin, when it is full-grown, gives birth to death." (James 1:12-15) It can feel a bit overwhelming reading all these verses, and it can make it sound like the world is just a giant trap waiting for us to fall into it. But the good news is God will be with us, and he will give us the power, creative ideas and help we need to overcome the temptations we face. And if we do give in, he will give us grace so we can get back up and keep going. Joseph was able to handle it, and with God's help, you will be able to as well.
questions: What do you think the biggest temptations young people your age are facing right now?
What sorts of things do you see your friends being tempted by?
Think back through your day yesterday, Was there any particular moments you experienced temptation? What was it? What was the temptation?

thoughts.verse: Stay alert! Watch out for your great enemy, the devil. He prowls around like a roaring lion, looking for someone to devour. Stand firm against him, and be strong in your faith.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you an awareness of the temptations that may trip you up, and ask him to give you creative ways to overcome them.

days.title: Refuse To Give In To Temptation
days.scripture: GENESIS 39:10 She kept putting pressure on Joseph day after day, but he refused to sleep with her, and he kept out of her way as much as possible.
days.reflection: The best way that a man can handle temptation is to aggressively resist it. Not just to half-heartedly try, but to resolve that he will not give in. Proverbs 6:5 says: "Save yourself like a gazelle escaping from a hunter, like a bird fleeing from a net." Have you ever seen a gazelle being hunted by lions on TV? They are running for their lives and bolting like crazy. They don’t stop to have a little snack, or say hello to the other animals. They do whatever it takes to get away. They twist; they turn; they leap; they escape. This is what Joseph did. He wasn’t wishy-washy. He didn’t mess around. This verse says HE REFUSED to sleep with her. To refuse means to reject, to oppose, to stop. It's a strong word, spoken by strong men. He didn’t entertain the option, think about how much pleasure it would be, or give her any space in his life. He shut the option out of his mind and focused on the work he had to do. Boys can tend to think that giving in to temptation isn’t that big of a deal because “it's just this one time,” but men know their character and destiny is being formed one choice at a time. They say no to the moment because of the kind of man they want to become. Temptation wants to start a sneaky conversation with you and get you to change your mind. It wants to lie to you and distort the truth. It says things like, “You can give in just this once. You don’t need to take things so seriously. No one will find out. It’s not that big a deal. You deserve this.” When we are tempted, our perception can get distorted. The pleasure seems massive and the consequences small. But when you give in to temptation, everything is reversed; the pleasure seems small and the consequences are massive. Just like a gazelle does everything within its power to escape the lions hunting it, Paul tells us that we need to do everything within our power to escape sin and temptation. He wrote this to a young leader named Timothy who was facing all sorts of difficulties leading a church in a city filled with temptation: "Run from anything that stimulates youthful lusts. Instead, pursue righteous living, faithfulness, love, and peace. Enjoy the companionship of those who call on the Lord with pure hearts." (2 Timothy 2:22) Don’t let temptation talk to you. Don’t let it walk along side you. Run, resist! The rewards of obedience will be worth it in the long run.
questions: When you were a little kid, was there a time you were tempted and gave in to it? What was it, and what happened?

Have you ever felt temptation trying to get into your head? Have you heard any of these thoughts come into your mind? “You can give in just this once. You don’t need to take things so seriously. No one will find out. It’s not that big a deal. You deserve this.” If so, which ones, and in what situation?

Martin Luther, a church leader from the Reformation, once said about temptation, “You cannot keep birds from flying over your head, but you can keep them from building a nest in your hair.” What do you think he meant by that?

thoughts.verse: So humble yourselves before God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Come close to God, and God will come close to you.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to strengthen your resolve to resist temptation. Ask him for help in any areas you may feel a bit weak in or are struggling with right now.

days.title: The Power Of Love And Fear
days.scripture: GENESIS 39:8-9 8 But Joseph refused. “Look,” he told her, “My master trusts me with everything in his entire household. 9 No one here has more authority than I do. He has held back nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. How could I do such a wicked thing? It would be a great sin against God.”
days.reflection: This week, you have been talking about the difficulty of temptation. You have been learning that boys yield, but men resist. But resisting things in real life is a lot harder than just reading about it here. There has to be some deep motivation in your heart if you're going to be able to resist temptation in the real world. This passage shows us the 2 things that motived Joseph to resist temptation. They were the source of power that enabled him to say no to Potiphar’s wife. The first thing we see is that Joseph LOVED GOD. Joseph wanted to be loyal to him. He wanted to please him. He wanted to live a life that reflected his will. He wasn’t just worried about getting caught; he was worried about grieving God's heart. He viewed giving in as a sin against God, not just the breaking of some sort of man-made law. He wanted to be faithful to God and not cheat on him or dishonor him. “How could I do such a wicked thing? It would be a great sin against God.” As you grow into a man, you will learn that true resistance must have a godward perspective and not just a consequence perspective. The second thing we see in Joseph is that he FEARED GOD. The fear of God is a term that can sometimes get people confused. It doesn’t mean that he was scared of God, but rather that he had reverence and respect for him. He knew that God was the one shaping and directing his life, and he took that seriously. He didn’t see God as his buddy but as his Lord. He had reverence for him, no matter what it cost. The fear of the Lord is important, because it helps us see life in its proper proportions. Proverbs 14:27 says, "Fear of the LORD is a life-giving fountain; it offers escape from the snares of death." Seeing God for who he is and honoring him gives us access to all the goodness and power that he has. And it keeps us from doing stupid things that get us in trouble, destroy our character, create shame, and separate us from him. The fear of God and Love of God are powerful forces. They enabled a teenager sold into slavery to withstand massive temptation, and they can do the same for you. No matter if the pressure is from friends at school, bullies online, or kids in your neighborhood, be loyal to God and don’t give in. And if you do, the fear of God will convict you, and the love of God will forgive you. How good is that?
questions: How does the love of God help when someone is tempted? How can fearing God help when someone is tempted?
Do you think that the things that young people are tempted with today are harder to resist than what Joseph was dealing with? Why or why not?
What are some of the things that you have done to be able to resist temptation?

thoughts.verse: God blesses those who patiently endure testing and temptation. Afterward they will receive the crown of life that God has promised to those who love him.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment and ask God to give you a deep love for him and his ways. Ask him to help you see him for who he is so you can grow in the fear of the Lord.

days.title: Take The Consequences Like A Man
days.scripture: GENESIS 39:2-6 10 She kept putting pressure on Joseph Day after day, but he refused to sleep with her, and he kept out of her way as much as possible. 11 One day, however, no one else was around when he went in to do his work. 12 She came and grabbed him by his cloak, demanding, “Come on, sleep with me!” Joseph tore himself away, but he left his cloak in her hand as he ran from the house. 13 When she saw that she was holding his cloak and he had fled, 14 she called out to her servants. Soon all the men came running. “Look!” she said. “My husband has brought this Hebrew slave here to make fools of us! He came into my room to rape me, but I screamed. 15 When he heard me scream, he ran outside and got away, but he left his cloak behind with me.” 16 She kept the cloak with her until her husband came home. 17 Then she told him her story. “That Hebrew slave you’ve brought into our house tried to come in and fool around with me,” she said. 18 “But when I screamed, he ran outside, leaving his cloak with me!” 19 Potiphar was furious when he heard his wife’s story about how Joseph had treated her. 20 So he took Joseph and threw him into the prison where the king’s prisoners were held, and there he remained.
days.reflection: In a magical world, Joseph resists temptation, obeys God, God waves his divine wand and the story ends. Joseph is whisked back home, everyone says sorry, he gets his coat back and gets married. He has kids, eats donuts, the end. But in real life, sometimes resisting temptation can cost us dearly. When we refuse to give in to what other people want, this can make them very angry. It can even make whole groups of people angry. This is important to note, because if we don’t accept the price of being obedient, we will give into temptation when things get hard, just so we can have an easier time. This week, we have looked at how Joseph resolved in his heart to obey God. His temptation came in the form of his boss’s wife trying to sleep with him. He didn’t ask for this intrusion, and he kept refusing in spite of how persistent she was. He used his love for God and hatred for sin to avoid her. Yet, Joseph's story is going to take a sad and hard turn. Potiphar’s wife is going to lie about Joseph and get him thrown in prison. His obedience to God and willingness to resist temptation all the way to the end will cost him his freedom. Joseph has been hated by his brothers, sold into slavery, worked his heart out, and all it got him was being thrown into prison. Doing the right thing can be hard. Doing the right thing can be costly. But it's better to take the heat for the right thing, than to give in and deal with the shame, consequences, and regret of doing what others want. And when you obey God and trust him to work out his plans for your life, you don’t have to freak out and think life is over. As you have learned, God can work things together for good. Even though Joseph is in prison and thinks his life may be over, God is going to reward him for his faithfulness by positioning him for his future. God will use this imprisonment as a springboard for his future. But as you are probably learning by now, he is going to have to overcome even more difficulties before it all comes together. Hebrews 2:18 says these encouraging words: "Since he himself has gone through suffering and testing; he is able to help us when we are being tested." Jesus faced the consequences of obedience and being faithful to his Father. He got worse than prison; he got the cross. Yet God used that horrible unjust death to bring us life and hope and joy. Jesus knows what it's like to handle the consequences of obedience, and if you ask him, he will help you be strong in those really tough moments.
questions: Have you ever done the right thing and then bad things happened as a result of it? What was the situation, and what happened?
How is Joseph building trust with God? Why do you think it's important to build trust with him?
Ask the person doing this study with you to share if they ever did the right thing and experienced something bad as a result of it.

thoughts.verse: So then, since we have a great High Priest who has entered heaven, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold firmly to what we believe. This High Priest of ours understands our weaknesses, for he faced all of the same testings we do, yet he did not sin. So let us come boldly to the throne of our gracious God. There we will receive his mercy, and we will find grace to help us when we need it most.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to help you have courage to obey him no matter what the cost. Ask him specifically for any challenge you may be having right now.

extras: WEEK 4 MANSCHOOL TEMPTATION ON THE TABLE OPTION 1. ACTIVITY One of the keys to resisting temptation is the idea of delayed gratification. You are probably familiar with the marshmallow experiment done in the seventies that highlights the power of waiting for rewards. You can read a bit more about it here. Find out what your son's favorite candy is and lay it on the bench on Monday morning. Tell him if he doesn’t touch it till Man School night, you will give him another bag. But if he eats any of them, he loses it. See how he does during the week. Then take him out for a meal and recap the week. OPTION 2. WATCH Watch this TED Talk on procrastination and instant gratification. Here's another helpful resource to help him understand how instant gratification works. OPTION 3. DINNER AND DISCUSSION Go out to one of your son's favorite restaurants and recap the week. Share from your own story (being appropriately vulnerable) about temptations you have faced. You may want to recap the big ideas: How temptation is intrusive. How we live in a world of constant temptation and need to be on guard. How to develop resolve to really resist temptation. Motivations to help you resist How to handle the consequences when the pressure is on. You may want to walk him through forgiveness, shame, and repentance when he gives into temptation.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
